title: Feuilleea portoricensis
taxonomy: Feuilleea portoricensis is classified in the family Annonaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. The genus Feuilleea is part of this family and is native to the Caribbean region. Within the Annonaceae family, Feuilleea portoricensis is placed in the order Magnoliales, which is a group of flowering plants that are characterized by their distinctive flower structures. The classification of Feuilleea portoricensis also includes the kingdom Plantae, which includes all plants, and the class Magnoliopsida, which includes flowering plants that have a dicotyledonous seed leaf arrangement. This comprehensive classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Feuilleea portoricensis within the plant kingdom.
